repair_history.diagnosis:
line 13: left and right sides of binary expression '*' are of type 'real' and 'Types::speed.speed_impl', but must be of the same type: multiplication of real and opaque type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' -> Change '1.0 * (Target - Actual)' to '(Target - Actual)'
line 13: left side of binary expression '-' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type 'int' or 'real': subtraction of opaque types 'Types::speed.speed_impl' -> Change 'Target - Actual' to '(Target - Actual)'
line 13: right side of binary expression '*' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type 'int' or 'real': multiplication of real and opaque type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' -> Change '0.0 * e_dot' to '0.0 * e_dot'
line 13: right side of binary expression '-' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type 'int' or 'real': subtraction of opaque types 'Types::speed.speed_impl' -> Change 'Target - Actual' to '(Target - Actual)'
line 14: Couldn't resolve reference to 'e': undefined variable 'e' in assume statement -> Change 'e = Target - Actual' to 'e = (Target - Actual)'

left and right sides of binary expression '+' are of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' and 'real', but must be of the same type: Port types 'Types::speed.speed_impl' used in arithmetic expressions without conversion to real -> Change 'Target' and 'Actual' to 'Target.value' and 'Actual.value' assuming they are scalar values of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' that can be accessed via a '.value' attribute
Couldn't resolve reference to 'e': Variable 'e' not declared in AGREE scope -> Remove or replace 'e' with direct expression '(Target - Actual)'
Couldn't resolve reference to 'integral': Function 'integral' not recognized in AGREE context -> Replace 'integral((Target - Actual))' with 'e_int' and define 'e_int' as a variable with proper initialization
Couldn't resolve reference to 'derivative': Function 'derivative' not recognized in AGREE context -> Replace 'derivative((Target - Actual))' with 'e_dot' and define 'e_dot' as a variable with proper initialization

line 13: Couldn't resolve reference to 'value'.: AGREE attempts to access 'value' attribute on AADL ports which do not support it -> Remove '.value' from Target and Actual in the guarantee statement
line 14: Couldn't resolve reference to 'e'.: Variable 'e' is not declared in the AGREE annex before use -> Move the assumption 'e = (Target - Actual)' to be an equation declaration
line 15: Couldn't resolve reference to 'integral'.: AGREE integral function not properly declared or referenced -> Replace 'integral(e)' with 'eq e_int : real = integral(e);' to properly declare the integral
line 16: Couldn't resolve reference to 'derivative'.: AGREE derivative function not properly declared or referenced -> Replace 'derivative(e)' with 'eq e_dot : real = derivative(e);' to properly declare the derivative

left and right sides of binary expression '*' are of type 'real' and 'Types::speed.speed_impl', but must be of the same type: Port types 'Types::speed.speed_impl' used in arithmetic expressions -> Change Target and Actual from port references to real-valued variables in the AGREE annex
left side of binary expression '-' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type 'int' or 'real': Port types 'Types::speed.speed_impl' used in arithmetic expressions -> Change Target and Actual from port references to real-valued variables in the AGREE annex
Couldn't resolve reference to 'integral'.: Integral function not recognized in AGREE context -> Replace integral(e) with a variable declaration and update the control law accordingly
Couldn't resolve reference to 'derivative'.: Derivative function not recognized in AGREE context -> Replace derivative(e) with a variable declaration and update the control law accordingly

left and right sides of binary expression '*' are of type 'real' and 'Types::speed.speed_impl', but must be of the same type: Port types 'Types::speed.speed_impl' used in arithmetic expressions without conversion to 'real' -> Change Target and Actual to be of type 'real' in the AGREE equations
left side of binary expression '-' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type 'int' or 'real': Port types 'Types::speed.speed_impl' used in arithmetic expressions without conversion to 'real' -> Change Target and Actual to be of type 'real' in the AGREE equations
